token_family_docs.$description: Fully resolved semantic color tokens — neutral, interaction, status, free, and brand. No mode resolution required. Light/Dark modes are pre-integrated — no manual mode switching needed. Use: Assign in component and HTML token files. Avoid: Referencing S1 tokens directly in components — S1 is a bridging layer where mode resolution is partial.

Official federal identity color (Bundesrot). Light/Dark modes apply — no emphasis modes. Use: Primary brand accent in navigation active states and official federal identity elements. Avoid: Interactive states or status feedback — use the interaction or status scale instead.

brand.$description: Federal identity color. Use for Bundesrot brand accent in navigation active states and primary brand elements.

token_family_docs.$description: Foundational colors for backgrounds, text, borders, and surfaces. Light/Dark modes apply — no emphasis modes. Use: Text, background, border, and shadow roles in standard UI components. Avoid: Interactive state feedback — use the interaction scale. Status communication — use the status scale.

no_color.$description: Static value when no color respectively 0% opacity is needed.

inversity_normal.$description: Foreground for Neutral. Lowest contrast: Use for placeholder text or minimum-visibility elements.

inversity_flipped.$description: Foreground for Neutral. Lowest contrast: Use for placeholder text or minimum-visibility elements.

inversity_normal.$description: Foreground for Neutral. Medium contrast: Use for secondary labels and supporting content.

inversity_flipped.$description: Foreground for Neutral. Medium contrast: Use for secondary labels and supporting content.

inversity_normal.$description: Foreground for Neutral. Low contrast: Use for supplementary or de-emphasized text; avoid where readable body text is needed.

inversity_flipped.$description: Foreground for Neutral. Low contrast: Use for supplementary or de-emphasized text; avoid where readable body text is needed.

inversity_normal.$description: Foreground for Neutral. High contrast: Use for body text, primary labels, and standard readable content.

inversity_flipped.$description: Foreground for Neutral. High contrast: Use for body text, primary labels, and standard readable content.

inversity_normal.$description: Foreground for Neutral. Highest contrast: Use for headings, primary content, and maximum legibility.

inversity_flipped.$description: Foreground for Neutral. Highest contrast: Use for headings, primary content, and maximum legibility.

inversity_normal.$description: Background for Neutral. Lowest contrast: Use for page backgrounds and canvas-level surfaces.

inversity_flipped.$description: Background for Neutral. Lowest contrast: Use for page backgrounds and canvas-level surfaces.

inversity_normal.$description: Background for Neutral. Highest contrast: Use for maximum-contrast surfaces; avoid on large areas that need to recede.

inversity_flipped.$description: Background for Neutral. Highest contrast: Use for maximum-contrast surfaces; avoid on large areas that need to recede.

inversity_normal.$description: Background for Neutral. High contrast: Use for distinct surfaces requiring strong background separation.

inversity_flipped.$description: Background for Neutral. High contrast: Use for distinct surfaces requiring strong background separation.

inversity_normal.$description: Background for Neutral. Medium contrast: Use for elevated surfaces and content containers.

inversity_flipped.$description: Background for Neutral. Medium contrast: Use for elevated surfaces and content containers.

inversity_normal.$description: Background for Neutral. Low contrast: Use for subtle surface differentiation and secondary areas.

inversity_flipped.$description: Background for Neutral. Low contrast: Use for subtle surface differentiation and secondary areas.

first.$description: Shadow for Neutral. Primary depth layer: Use for near-surface elevation on raised elements; adapts to light and dark mode.

second.$description: Shadow for Neutral. Secondary depth layer: Use for deeper elevation or layered shadows on raised elements; adapts to light and dark mode.

inversity_normal.$description: Border for Neutral. Subtle strength: Use for low-emphasis dividers and structural separators.

inversity_flipped.$description: Border for Neutral. Subtle strength: Use for low-emphasis dividers and structural separators.

inversity_normal.$description: Border for Neutral. Medium strength: Use for standard form controls, table cells, and contained areas.

inversity_flipped.$description: Border for Neutral. Medium strength: Use for standard form controls, table cells, and contained areas.

inversity_normal.$description: Border for Neutral. Strong strength: Use for focus outlines, selected states, and maximum-emphasis borders.

inversity_flipped.$description: Border for Neutral. Strong strength: Use for focus outlines, selected states, and maximum-emphasis borders.

token_family_docs.$description: Interactive state colors for components. Light/Dark and High/Low emphasis modes apply — all 4 mode combinations resolve to distinct values. Use: Interactive component states — buttons, links, form controls, and focusable elements. Avoid: Static decorative elements. Status communication — use the status scale instead.

inversity_normal.$description: Foreground for Interaction. Enabled state: Use for text and icons in the default, ready-to-interact state.

inversity_flipped.$description: Foreground for Interaction. Enabled state: Use for text and icons in the default, ready-to-interact state.

inversity_normal.$description: Foreground for Interaction. Hover state: Use for text and icons when a pointer enters the interactive area.

inversity_flipped.$description: Foreground for Interaction. Hover state: Use for text and icons when a pointer enters the interactive area.

inversity_normal.$description: Foreground for Interaction. Focus state: Use for text and icons when an element receives keyboard focus.

inversity_flipped.$description: Foreground for Interaction. Focus state: Use for text and icons when an element receives keyboard focus.

inversity_normal.$description: Foreground for Interaction. Pressed state: Use for text and icons during an active click or tap.

inversity_flipped.$description: Foreground for Interaction. Pressed state: Use for text and icons during an active click or tap.

inversity_normal.$description: Foreground for Interaction. Selected state: Use for text and icons on a currently selected or active element.

inversity_flipped.$description: Foreground for Interaction. Selected state: Use for text and icons on a currently selected or active element.

inversity_normal.$description: Foreground for Interaction. Disabled state: Use for text and icons on elements that are not available for interaction.

inversity_flipped.$description: Foreground for Interaction. Disabled state: Use for text and icons on elements that are not available for interaction.

inversity_normal.$description: Foreground for Interaction. Visited state: Use for text and icons on links or elements that have already been activated.

inversity_flipped.$description: Foreground for Interaction. Visited state: Use for text and icons on links or elements that have already been activated.

selected.$description: Border for Interaction. Selected state: Use for selection indicators and active border elements. Note: currently references federal_red — pending color decision.

inversity_normal.$description: Background for Interaction. Enabled state: Use for surfaces in the default, ready-to-interact state.

inversity_flipped.$description: Background for Interaction. Enabled state: Use for surfaces in the default, ready-to-interact state.

inversity_normal.$description: Background for Interaction. Hover state: Use for surfaces when a pointer enters the interactive area.

inversity_flipped.$description: Background for Interaction. Hover state: Use for surfaces when a pointer enters the interactive area.

inversity_normal.$description: Background for Interaction. Focus state: Use for surfaces when an element receives keyboard focus.

inversity_flipped.$description: Background for Interaction. Focus state: Use for surfaces when an element receives keyboard focus.

inversity_normal.$description: Background for Interaction. Pressed state: Use for surfaces during an active click or tap.

inversity_flipped.$description: Background for Interaction. Pressed state: Use for surfaces during an active click or tap.

inversity_normal.$description: Background for Interaction. Selected state: Use for surfaces on a currently selected or active element.

inversity_flipped.$description: Background for Interaction. Selected state: Use for surfaces on a currently selected or active element.

inversity_normal.$description: Background for Interaction. Visited state: Use for surfaces behind links or elements that have already been activated.

inversity_flipped.$description: Background for Interaction. Visited state: Use for surfaces behind links or elements that have already been activated.

inversity_normal.$description: Background for Interaction. Disabled state: Use for surfaces on elements that are not available for interaction.

inversity_flipped.$description: Background for Interaction. Disabled state: Use for surfaces on elements that are not available for interaction.

inversity_normal.$description: Focus ring for Interaction. Use on all focusable elements to communicate keyboard focus; must remain visible against any surface.

inversity_flipped.$description: Focus ring for Interaction. Use on all focusable elements to communicate keyboard focus; must remain visible against any surface.

token_family_docs.$description: Status communication colors. Contrast levels (e.g. contrast_highest) are available. Light/Dark modes apply — no emphasis modes. Use: Alerts, badges, and any element that communicates system or process state. Avoid: Decoration or branding — status colors carry fixed semantic meaning. Interactive states — use the interaction scale.

inversity_normal.$description: Background for Info status. Highest contrast: Use for large status surfaces, prominent info areas; avoid when instant color hue recognition is critical — highest contrast reduces chroma and weakens the categorical status signal.

inversity_flipped.$description: Background for Info status. Highest contrast: Use for large status surfaces, prominent info areas; avoid when instant color hue recognition is critical — highest contrast reduces chroma and weakens the categorical status signal.

inversity_normal.$description: Background for Info status. High contrast: Use for status containers, highlighted info surfaces.

inversity_flipped.$description: Background for Info status. High contrast: Use for status containers, highlighted info surfaces.

inversity_normal.$description: Background for Info status. Medium contrast: Use for subtle info surfaces, secondary status areas.

inversity_flipped.$description: Background for Info status. Medium contrast: Use for subtle info surfaces, secondary status areas.

inversity_normal.$description: Background for Info status. Low contrast: Use for compact status elements, small surfaces; avoid surfaces with extended text — high saturation compensates for lower contrast.

inversity_flipped.$description: Background for Info status. Low contrast: Use for compact status elements, small surfaces; avoid surfaces with extended text — high saturation compensates for lower contrast. Flipped inversity: use on elements that must visually stick out.

inversity_flipped.$description: Foreground for Info status. Highest contrast: Use for primary text and icons on Info status surfaces.

token_family_docs.$description: Free colors — no predefined semantic role. Contrast levels (e.g. contrast_highest) are available. Light/Dark modes apply — no emphasis modes. Use: Tags, badges, and contextual color accents. Suitable for categorical distinction. Avoid: Substituting for status or interaction colors — free tokens carry no fixed semantic meaning.

inversity_flipped.$description: Foreground for Free – Indigo. Medium contrast: Use for secondary text communicating in-progress or review state.

inversity_normal.$description: Foreground for Free – Indigo. Low contrast: Use for non-essential text or icons communicating in-progress or review state; avoid if highest readability for extended text is required.

inversity_flipped.$description: Foreground for Free – Indigo. Low contrast: Use for non-essential text or icons communicating in-progress or review state; avoid if highest readability for extended text is required.

inversity_normal.$description: Background for Free – Pink. Highest contrast: Use on surfaces communicating scheduled or upcoming state; avoid when instant hue recognition is critical — highest contrast reduces chroma.

inversity_flipped.$description: Background for Free – Pink. Highest contrast: Use on surfaces communicating scheduled or upcoming state; avoid when instant hue recognition is critical — highest contrast reduces chroma.

inversity_normal.$description: Background for Free – Pink. High contrast: Use on containers and areas communicating scheduled or upcoming state.

inversity_flipped.$description: Background for Free – Pink. High contrast: Use on containers and areas communicating scheduled or upcoming state.

inversity_normal.$description: Background for Free – Pink. Medium contrast: Use on subtle surfaces communicating scheduled or upcoming state.

inversity_flipped.$description: Background for Free – Pink. Medium contrast: Use on subtle surfaces communicating scheduled or upcoming state.

inversity_normal.$description: Background for Free – Pink. Low contrast: Use on compact elements communicating scheduled or upcoming state; avoid surfaces with extended text — high saturation compensates for lower contrast.

inversity_flipped.$description: Background for Free – Pink. Low contrast: Use on compact elements communicating scheduled or upcoming state; avoid surfaces with extended text — high saturation compensates for lower contrast. Flipped inversity: use on elements that must visually stick out.

inversity_normal.$description: Foreground for Free – Pink. Highest contrast: Use for primary text and icons communicating scheduled or upcoming state.

inversity_flipped.$description: Foreground for Free – Pink. Highest contrast: Use for primary text and icons communicating scheduled or upcoming state.

inversity_normal.$description: Foreground for Free – Pink. High contrast: Use for labels and icons communicating scheduled or upcoming state.

inversity_flipped.$description: Foreground for Free – Pink. High contrast: Use for labels and icons communicating scheduled or upcoming state.

inversity_normal.$description: Foreground for Free – Pink. Medium contrast: Use for secondary text communicating scheduled or upcoming state.

inversity_flipped.$description: Foreground for Free – Pink. Medium contrast: Use for secondary text communicating scheduled or upcoming state.

inversity_normal.$description: Foreground for Free – Pink. Low contrast: Use for non-essential text or icons communicating scheduled or upcoming state; avoid if highest readability for extended text is required.

inversity_flipped.$description: Foreground for Free – Pink. Low contrast: Use for non-essential text or icons communicating scheduled or upcoming state; avoid if highest readability for extended text is required.

token_family_docs.$description: Navigation color tokens. Used for top navigation bar, left navigation panel, and tab components. Emphasis mode (high/low) has no effect — navigation items use a single color scale based on the brand red palette.

enabled.$description: Foreground for Navigation. Enabled state: Use for text and icons in the default, ready-to-interact state.

hover.$description: Foreground for Navigation. Hover state: Use for text and icons when a pointer enters the navigation item.

focus.$description: Foreground for Navigation. Focus state: Use for text and icons when a navigation item receives keyboard focus.

pressed.$description: Foreground for Navigation. Pressed state: Use for text and icons during an active click or tap on a navigation item.

selected.$description: Foreground for Navigation. Selected state: Use for text and icons on the currently active navigation item.

disabled.$description: Foreground for Navigation. Disabled state: Use for text and icons on navigation items not available for interaction.

enabled.$description: Background for Navigation. Enabled state: Use for the surface of a navigation item in its default state.

hover.$description: Background for Navigation. Hover state: Use for the surface of a navigation item when a pointer enters it.

focus.$description: Background for Navigation. Focus state: Use for the surface of a navigation item that receives keyboard focus.

pressed.$description: Background for Navigation. Pressed state: Use for the surface of a navigation item during an active click or tap.

selected.$description: Background for Navigation. Selected state: Use for the surface of the currently active navigation item.

disabled.$description: Background for Navigation. Disabled state: Use for the surface of navigation items not available for interaction.

enabled.$description: Border for Navigation. Enabled state: Subtle indicator line on navigation items in the default state.

hover.$description: Border for Navigation. Hover state: Subtle indicator line on navigation items when a pointer enters.

focus.$description: Border for Navigation. Focus state: Subtle indicator line on navigation items that receive keyboard focus.

pressed.$description: Border for Navigation. Pressed state: Brand-red indicator line during an active click or tap.

selected.$description: Border for Navigation. Selected state: Brand-red indicator line marking the currently active navigation item. Uses ob.p.color.basic.federal_red.

disabled.$description: Border for Navigation. Disabled state: Muted indicator line on navigation items not available for interaction.

focus_ring.$description: Focus ring for Navigation. Use on all focusable navigation items to communicate keyboard focus; must remain visible against any surface.
